summary: openai-agents-helpers postinstall harvests installer identity, SSH/AWS/GCP/GitHub metadata to remote endpoint
details: This package impersonates the OpenAI Agents SDK ecosystem but ships no real library code — its declared main `dist/index.js` is absent from the tarball. Its sole effect on install is `scripts/postinstall.js`, which enumerates installer-side identity and cloud-footprint data and POSTs it to a hardcoded remote endpoint. Specifically, the postinstall reads `~/.gitconfig` and `.git/config` (user.email, reflog committer emails), `~/.ssh/*.pub` key comment emails, `~/.config/gh/hosts.yml` (GitHub CLI identity), `~/.config/gcloud/properties` (GCP project/account), `~/.aws/config` (AWS profile names, SSO URLs), DNS search domain, CI provider, hostname/username/platform via `os.hostname()`/`os.userInfo()`/`os.platform()`, and parent-project `package.json` metadata. The collected JSON blob is sent via `https.request` to `npm-package-logger-228835561205.europe-west1.run.app`. Comments in the script frame this as anonymous diagnostics, but the payload is precisely the installer's personal and corporate identity graph — SCM identity, cloud account identifiers, SSH key owner emails, and repo context — harvested from paths the package did not create and has no legitimate reason to read. The `author` field points to `openai-agents-js.guide`, an unrelated domain, not OpenAI.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
